items.title: No primitive roles at project level
items.description: Avoid owner/editor/viewer; use least-privilege custom roles. This control is evaluated under "IAM and Organization" during security assessments. Fixing this improves defense-in-depth and long-term resilience. Document affected assets, evidence, and business owners when reporting.
items.how_to_find: GCP portal → IAM and Organization. Audit configuration against "No primitive roles at project level". CLI: use `gcloud` to list resources and IAM bindings. Run Prowler (`prowler gcp`) or Defender/Security Command Center for misconfig findings.
items.how_to_fix: Avoid owner/editor/viewer; use least-privilege custom roles. Apply CIS benchmark controls: least-privilege IAM, private endpoints, encryption with customer-managed keys, centralized logging, and alert rules for policy changes. Document exceptions and set remediation SLAs by severity.

items.title: Service account key hygiene
items.description: Rotate keys, avoid user-managed keys where workload identity works. This control is evaluated under "IAM and Organization" during security assessments. Fixing this improves defense-in-depth and long-term resilience. Document affected assets, evidence, and business owners when reporting.

items.how_to_fix: Rotate keys, avoid user-managed keys where workload identity works. items.title: Organization policies enforced
items.description: Restrict public IPs, require OS Login, and domain-restricted sharing. This control is evaluated under "IAM and Organization" during security assessments. If exploited, this often leads to full account or environment compromise and must be treated as a release blocker. Document affected assets, evidence, and business owners when reporting.
items.how_to_find: GCP portal → IAM and Organization. Audit configuration against "Organization policies enforced". CLI: use `gcloud` to list resources and IAM bindings. Run Prowler (`prowler gcp`) or Defender/Security Command Center for misconfig findings.

Document exceptions and set remediation SLAs by severity.
